Scenario.title: J-Magic Campaign Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple This scenario depicts a targeted attack against organizations in the Healthcare and Public Health sector, leveraging expert-level tactics to disrupt services through network compromise.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Potentially seeking to cause service disruptions or gather sensitive information due to the high-stakes nature of healthcare networks.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Script File, Target Artifact (Stored)
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Process Creation (Mechanism), Stored Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2013-2597: Stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in Code Aurora audio driver allowing privilege escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-220: Client-Server Protocol Manipulation – Exploiting weaknesses in communication protocols to perform unauthorized actions
stage.description: The attacker gains initial entry by exploiting a well-known vulnerability in Juniper routers' management interfaces, specifically targeting configurations that allow remote access. This is achieved through a delivery method involving crafted network packets sent over the internet, compromising exposed devices common in healthcare settings.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2020-5410: Path traversal vulnerability in Spring Cloud Config allowing arbitrary file access
stage.capec: CAPEC-532: Altered Installed BIOS – Sending malicious updates to maintain persistent access
stage.description: Upon successful initial access, the attacker executes a malicious script obtained from the compromised system. This script, likely modified from variants associated with the J-magic Campaign, leverages process creation mechanisms to run automatically, enabling further commands and ensuring the foothold remains active for ongoing operations.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-153: Input Data Manipulation – Controlling input formats to alter system behavior or data integrity
stage.description: The attackers manipulate stored data within the affected router or network artifacts, leading to significant disruption. This could involve rerouting network traffic to deny legitimate services or initiating distributed denial-of-service attacks using amplified ICMP floods, thereby impacting critical communications and operations in healthcare environments.

Scenario.summary: The J-magic Campaign targets healthcare IT infrastructure with a straightforward attack chain focusing on exploiting vulnerable internet-facing devices for initial access, executing scripts for persistence, and causing operational impacts through data manipulation.
